```python
# test_parse_csv_line.py

import pytest

from your_module import parse_csv_line  # Replace with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "line, expected",
    [
        ("a,b,c", ["a", "b", "c"]),
        ("one,two,three", ["one", "two", "three"]),
        ("hello", ["hello"]),
        ('"a","b","c"', ["a", "b", "c"]),
        ('a,"b,c",d', ["a", "b,c", "d"]),
        ('"a,b,c"', ["a,b,c"]),
        ('a,"b,c,d",e', ["a", "b,c,d", "e"]),
    ],
)
def test_parse_csv_line_happy_path(line, expected):
    assert parse_csv_line(line) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "line, expected",
    [
        ("", [""]),
        (",", ["", ""]),
        (",,", ["", "", ""]),
        ("a,,c", ["a", "", "c"]),
        (",a,b", ["", "a", "b"]),
        ("a,b,", ["a", "b", ""]),
        (",a,", ["", "a", ""]),
        ('""', [""]),
        ('a,"",c', ["a", "", "c"]),
        ("   ", ["   "]),
        (" a , b , c ", [" a ", " b ", " c "]),
        (' a ," b,c ", d ', [" a ", " b,c ", " d "]),
    ],
)
def test_parse_csv_line_edge_cases(line, expected):
    assert parse_csv_line(line) == expected


def test_parse_csv_line_preserves_newline_characters():
    assert parse_csv_line("a,b\n") == ["a", "b\n"]


def test_parse_csv_line_allows_commas_inside_quotes():
    assert parse_csv_line('name,"hello, world",active') == [
        "name",
        "hello, world",
        "active",
    ]


def test_parse_csv_line_removes_quote_characters():
    assert parse_csv_line('"abc",def') == ["abc", "def"]


def test_parse_csv_line_unmatched_quote_does_not_raise():
    """
    The current implementation does not validate balanced quotes.
    It simply toggles quote state and returns the parsed result.
    """
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b,c') == ["a", "b,c"]


def test_parse_csv_line_escaped_quotes_are_not_supported():
    """
    Standard CSV escaped quotes are represented as "" inside a quoted field.
    This parser does not support that behavior; it treats each quote as a
    toggle and removes them.
    """
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b""c",d') == ["a", "bc", "d"]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [None, 123, b"a,b,c"])
def test_parse_csv_line_invalid_input_type_raises_type_error(bad_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        parse_csv_line(bad_input)
```
